entries.name: Great Barrier Reef
entries.desc: The world's largest coral reef system off Queensland, visible from space and a UNESCO World Heritage site. Outer reefs offer clearer water and encounters with dwarf minke whales in winter.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Arlington Reef, part of the Great Barrier Reef, Queensland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: great-barrier-reef.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Arlington-Reef-2018-aerial-view-Luka-Peternel" by Luka Peternel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Arlington-Reef-2018-aerial-view-Luka-Peternel.jpg -->

entries.name: Ningaloo Reef
entries.desc: A fringing reef along Western Australia's coast, reachable from shore. It is one of the most reliable places to swim with whale sharks during their seasonal aggregation.

photo.alt: Whale shark encountered by divers at Ningaloo Reef, Western Australia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ningaloo-whale-shark.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Requin baleine ningaloo reef" by Chewb62 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Requin_baleine_ningaloo_reef.JPG -->

entries.name: Tiger Beach
entries.desc: A shallow sandy site off Grand Bahama known for close encounters with tiger sharks in bright, clear water. It is one of the world's best-known shark-diving locations.

photo.alt: Tiger shark at Tiger Beach off Grand Bahama
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tiger-shark-tiger-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Tiger Shark At Tigerbeach Bahamas (149044369)" by Marion Kraschl · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tiger_Shark_At_Tigerbeach_Bahamas_(149044369).jpeg -->

entries.name: Great Blue Hole
entries.desc: A large circular marine sinkhole off the Belize coast, part of the Belize Barrier Reef. Its deep walls hold ancient limestone stalactites formed when the cavern was above sea level.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the Great Blue Hole marine sinkhole off the coast of Belize
photo.credit: <!-- photo: great-blue-hole.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Belize Blue Hole (TMP) (16912331906)" by The TerraMar Project · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Belize_Blue_Hole_(TMP)_(16912331906).jpg -->

entries.name: Bonaire
entries.desc: A Dutch Caribbean island whose entire coastline is a protected marine park. It is renowned for accessible shore diving, with dozens of reef sites entered directly from the beach.

photo.alt: Diver at the top of the 1000 Steps shore-diving site, Bonaire
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bonaire-1000-steps.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Bonaire 1000 steps" by janderk · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bonaire_1000_steps.jpg -->

entries.name: Stingray City
entries.desc: A shallow sandbar in Grand Cayman's North Sound where wild southern stingrays gather. The waist-deep water makes it accessible to snorkelers and novice divers alike.

photo.alt: Southern stingrays gathering in shallow water at Stingray City, Grand Cayman
photo.credit: <!-- photo: stingray-city-grand-cayman.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Stingray City, Grand Cayman - 54289289499" by Symbiosis · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Stingray_City,_Grand_Cayman_-_54289289499.jpg -->

entries.name: Cocos Island
entries.desc: An uninhabited Pacific island reached only by multi-day liveaboard, protected as a national park. Its currents draw large schools of scalloped hammerhead sharks.

photo.alt: Scalloped hammerhead shark near Cocos Island, Costa Rica
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hammerhead-shark-cocos-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Hammerhead shark, Cocos Island, Costa Rica" by Barry Peters · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hammerhead_shark,_Cocos_Island,_Costa_Rica.jpg -->

entries.name: Galápagos Islands
entries.desc: A volcanic archipelago on the equator where cool nutrient-rich currents support unusual marine life. Remote northern islands are known for large hammerhead schools and marine iguanas.

photo.alt: School of scalloped hammerhead sharks at Wolf Island, Galápagos
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hammerhead-shark-galapagos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Sphyrna lewini galapagos 2" by Anthony Patterson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sphyrna_lewini_galapagos_2.jpg -->

entries.name: Ras Mohammed
entries.desc: A national park at the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula where the Red Sea meets deep water. Sheer coral walls and seasonal fish aggregations make it a Red Sea highlight.

photo.alt: Rocky headland and reef flat at Ras Mohammed National Park where the shallow reef drops into deep Red Sea water
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ras-mohammed-coral-reef.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Coral reef in Ras Muhammad nature park.JPG" by ssr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Coral_reef_in_Ras_Muhammad_nature_park.JPG -->

entries.name: SS Thistlegorm
entries.desc: A British merchant ship sunk in the Red Sea in 1941, its holds still packed with wartime cargo of trucks, motorcycles, and munitions. It ranks among the world's great wreck dives.

photo.alt: Coral-encrusted wreckage of the SS Thistlegorm on the Red Sea floor
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ss-thistlegorm-wreck.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Aft view Thistlegorm.jpg" by Woodym555 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aft_view_Thistlegorm.jpg -->

entries.name: Beqa Lagoon
entries.desc: A large barrier-reef lagoon south of Fiji's main island, best known for organized shark dives that gather bull sharks and other species alongside vivid soft-coral reefs.

photo.alt: Bull shark swimming over the reef in Beqa Lagoon, Fiji
photo.credit: <!-- photo: beqa-lagoon-bull-shark.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Bullshark Beqa Fiji 2007.jpg" by Pterantula · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bullshark_Beqa_Fiji_2007.jpg -->

entries.name: Fakarava
entries.desc: A Tuamotu atoll and UNESCO biosphere reserve whose southern pass hosts a dense wall of grey reef sharks, a phenomenon that peaks during the annual grouper spawning.

photo.alt: Turquoise lagoon and palm-lined motu at Fakarava atoll, French Polynesia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fakarava-lagoon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Fakarava - Lagon vert au motu Farakau.jpg" by Fanny98716 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fakarava_-_Lagon_vert_au_motu_Farakau.jpg -->

entries.name: Roatán
entries.desc: The largest of the Bay Islands, ringed by the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef. Steep coral walls and warm clear water make it a long-established Caribbean dive destination.

photo.alt: Clear turquoise reef water off the coast of Roatán, Honduras
photo.credit: <!-- photo: roatan-coral-reef.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Arrecife coralino de Roatán.jpg" by Luis Alfredo Romero · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Arrecife_coralino_de_Roat%C3%A1n.jpg -->

entries.name: Silfra Fissure
entries.desc: A rift in Thingvellir National Park where divers move between the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates. Filtered glacial meltwater gives extraordinary underwater visibility.

photo.alt: Diver's bubbles rising through the crystal-clear rift walls of Silfra fissure
photo.credit: <!-- photo: silfra-fissure-diver.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "SCUBA Silfra.jpg" by Thomei08 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SCUBA_Silfra.jpg -->

entries.name: Raja Ampat
entries.desc: An archipelago in West Papua at the heart of the Coral Triangle, holding some of the richest marine biodiversity on Earth, from tiny critters to manta rays and reef sharks.

photo.alt: Diver over a coral reef teeming with reef fish in Raja Ampat
photo.credit: <!-- photo: raja-ampat-piaynemo-viewpoint.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Pulau Piaynemo, Raja Ampat.jpg" by Rizalubun · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pulau_Piaynemo,_Raja_Ampat.jpg -->

entries.name: Komodo National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site in the Lesser Sunda Islands with strong currents that draw manta rays and large pelagics. Northern reefs are warm while cooler southern sites support dense soft coral.

photo.alt: A reef manta ray gliding over the seafloor in Komodo National Park, Indonesia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: komodo-manta-ray.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "A Flying Manta ray" by Siharaditia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_Flying_Manta_ray.jpg -->

entries.name: Sipadan Island
entries.desc: An oceanic island off Borneo rising from deep water, famous for sea turtles and swirling schools of barracuda and jackfish. Daily diver numbers are capped by permit.

photo.alt: Sipadan Island rising from the Celebes Sea off Borneo, Malaysia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sipadan-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Pulau Sipadan.jpg" by Bernard DUPONT · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pulau_Sipadan.jpg -->

entries.name: South Ari Atoll
entries.desc: A Maldivian atoll whose protected zone hosts one of the world's few resident whale shark populations, alongside manta rays drawn to its plankton-rich channels.

photo.alt: Beach and water villas at Thudufushi, South Ari Atoll, Maldives
photo.credit: <!-- photo: thudufushi-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Diamonds Thudufushi Beach and Water Villas, May 2017 -08.jpg" by Martin Falbisoner · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Diamonds_Thudufushi_Beach_and_Water_Villas,_May_2017_-08.jpg -->

entries.name: Gozo
entries.desc: The smaller of Malta's main islands, offering clear Mediterranean water with caves, arches, and swim-throughs. Several WWII-era wrecks lie within easy reach of shore.

photo.alt: Fungus Rock in Dwejra Bay, Gozo, Malta
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fungus-rock-dwejra-gozo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Fungus Rock, Dwejra Bay, Gozo.jpg" by Bogdan Giușcă · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fungus_Rock,_Dwejra_Bay,_Gozo.jpg -->

entries.name: Cozumel
entries.desc: An island off the Yucatán coast known for fast drift dives along the Palancar and Colombia reefs, part of a protected marine park with vivid sponge and coral formations.

photo.alt: Coastal view at Punta Sur, Cozumel, Mexico
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cozumel-punta-sur.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Cozumel Punta Sur View-27527.jpg" by Ken Thomas · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cozumel_Punta_Sur_View-27527.jpg -->

entries.name: Cenotes
entries.desc: Freshwater sinkholes connected to the peninsula's vast flooded cave systems. They offer exceptional clarity and haloclines where fresh and salt water meet, a specialty of cavern and cave divers.

photo.alt: Cenote Ik-Kil, a limestone sinkhole in Yucatán, Mexico
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cenote-ik-kil.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cenote Ik-Kil.jpg" by Laredawg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cenote_Ik-Kil.jpg -->

entries.name: Socorro Islands
entries.desc: A volcanic group in the Revillagigedo archipelago far off mainland Mexico, reached by liveaboard. It is renowned for curious giant oceanic manta rays and winter humpback whales.

photo.alt: Socorro Island in the Revillagigedo Archipelago, Mexico
photo.credit: <!-- photo: isla-socorro.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Isla Socorro.jpg" by México en Fotos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Isla_Socorro.jpg -->

entries.name: Truk Lagoon
entries.desc: A sheltered lagoon holding the sunken remains of a Japanese wartime fleet lost in 1944. The wrecks, now coral-encrusted, form one of the world's premier wreck-diving sites.

photo.alt: Deck of the sunken Fujikawa Maru wreck, Truk Lagoon, Chuuk
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fujikawa-maru-wreck.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Deck of Fujikawa maru.jpg" by Motoki Kurabayashi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Deck_of_Fujikawa_maru.jpg -->

entries.name: Blue Corner
entries.desc: A current-swept reef plateau where divers hook onto the reef edge to watch sharks and large fish pass. It is Palau's signature dive and among the most celebrated in the Pacific.

photo.alt: Rock Islands near German Channel, adjacent to Blue Corner, Palau
photo.credit: <!-- photo: rock-islands-german-channel-palau.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Rock Islands near German Channel, Palau (9971717656).jpg" by Matt Kieffer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Rock_Islands_near_German_Channel,_Palau_(9971717656).jpg -->

entries.name: Kimbe Bay
entries.desc: A deep bay on New Britain within the Coral Triangle, dotted with offshore seamounts and pinnacles. It is noted for high coral diversity and clear, sheltered water.

photo.alt: Restorf Island in Kimbe Bay, Papua New Guinea
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kimbe-bay-islands.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Kimbe Bay islands.jpg" by Kimbe · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kimbe_Bay_islands.jpg -->

entries.name: Tubbataha Reef
entries.desc: A remote UNESCO marine park in the Sulu Sea, reachable only by liveaboard during a short season. Its isolated atolls host sharks, turtles, and steep coral walls.

photo.alt: Terns resting on a sandbar in Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tubbataha-reef.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO · "Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park-112057.jpg" by Ron Van Oers · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tubbataha_Reefs_Natural_Park-112057.jpg -->

entries.name: Malapascua
entries.desc: A small island north of Cebu, one of the few places where thresher sharks are reliably seen at a cleaning station on the nearby Monad Shoal, best at dawn.

photo.alt: Bounty Beach on Malapascua Island, Philippines
photo.credit: <!-- photo: malapascua-bounty-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Malapascua (island), Bounty Beach, Philippines.jpg" by Vyacheslav Argenberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Malapascua_(island),_Bounty_Beach,_Philippines.jpg -->

entries.name: Azores
entries.desc: A volcanic archipelago in the mid-Atlantic where offshore seamounts draw pelagic life. Summer diving features blue sharks, mobula rays, and dramatic underwater rock formations.

photo.alt: Vila Franca Islet, a volcanic crater islet off São Miguel Island, Azores
photo.credit: <!-- photo: azores-vila-franca-islet.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Vila Franca Islet with Vila Franca do Campo in the background, São Miguel Island, Azores, Portugal (PPL1-Corrected).jpg" by Jules Verne Times Two · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vila_Franca_Islet_with_Vila_Franca_do_Campo_in_the_background,_S%C3%A3o_Miguel_Island,_Azores,_Portugal_(PPL1-Corrected).jpg -->

entries.name: Aldabra Atoll
entries.desc: A remote UNESCO World Heritage atoll, one of the largest raised coral atolls on Earth. Its isolation keeps reefs pristine, with reef sharks, mantas, and giant tortoises above water.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Aldabra Atoll's lagoon, Seychelles
photo.credit: <!-- photo: aldabra-atoll-aerial.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Seychelles outer islands 25.08.2009 10-19-35.jpg" by Simisa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seychelles_outer_islands_25.08.2009_10-19-35.jpg -->

entries.name: Sodwana Bay
entries.desc: Part of the iSimangaliso Wetland Park on South Africa's east coast, marking the southernmost tropical coral reefs in Africa. Nearby canyons famously shelter living coelacanths.

photo.alt: Dune path down to the beach at Sodwana Bay, South Africa
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sodwana-bay-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Sodwana Beach ... (Photo JC PLE) - 52313083832.jpg" by Bernard DUPONT · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sodwana_Beach_..._(Photo_JC_PLE)_-_52313083832.jpg -->

entries.name: Medes Islands
entries.desc: A small protected archipelago off the Costa Brava, one of the Mediterranean's oldest marine reserves. Decades of protection have restored abundant groupers and other reef fish.

photo.alt: The Medes Islands off the Costa Brava, Spain
photo.credit: <!-- photo: medes-islands.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Spain, Catalonia, Illes Medes (Medes Islands).JPG" by Vincent van Zeijst · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Spain,_Catalonia,_Illes_Medes_(Medes_Islands).JPG -->

entries.name: Similan Islands
entries.desc: A protected island group in the Andaman Sea off Phuket, known for granite boulder formations and clear water. The park closes seasonally to allow reefs to recover.

photo.alt: Sail Rock granite boulder formation in the Similan Islands, Thailand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: similan-islands-rock-formation.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "SIMILAN Landmark.jpg" by Chowalit · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SIMILAN_Landmark.jpg -->
